entities.name: Mark Cerny
entities.description:
Lead/central architect of the PlayStation console line who implemented a two-year feedback cycle and influenced PS5 priorities.

entities.name: two-year feedback cycle
entities.description: Regularly visiting Sony's first-party developers at two-year intervals to gather concerns and improvement suggestions for console hardware.

entities.name: first-party developers
entities.description: Development teams employed or contracted by Sony providing feedback on hardware needs and concerns.

entities.name: loading times
entities.description: Length of time games take to load or stream new game areas; a central challenge the PS5 aimed to address.

entities.name: hard disk drive I/O speed
aliases: standard I/O speed of a hard disk drive
entities.description: Data transfer rate of traditional hard disk drives cited as a limiting factor for game development.

entities.name: data rates
entities.description: Speed at which game data can be read from storage; low rates limit size and placement of game data and increase duplication to reduce load times.

entities.name: Epic Games
entities.description: A game developer (company) referenced as providing feedback about storage limits (represented by Tim Sweeney).

entities.name: Tim Sweeney
entities.description: Representative from Epic Games who informed Mark Cerny that HDD I/O speed was a limiting factor.

entities.name: streaming/dynamic loading
entities.description: Technique where games stream or dynamically load new areas as the player moves, sensitive to storage I/O performance.

entities.name: duplication of data
entities.description: Storing multiple copies of data across storage medium to reduce load times when I/O speeds are limited.

entities.name: game data physical layout
aliases: physical location of data on the storage medium
entities.description: Where data is placed on storage; limited by data rates and affects load times.

entities.name: reduce loading time
entities.description: Goal/effort to minimize how long games take to load, particularly important for streaming/dynamic loading scenarios.

entities.name: development team
entities.description: The group responsible for PS5 hardware priorities influenced by developer feedback.

entities.name: game development limits
entities.description: Constraints on what developers can do (size of data loaded, data placement, duplication) caused by slow storage I/O.

entities.description: A hypothetical lower-cost, lower-spec variant of the PlayStation 5 discussed as a concept similar to Xbox Series S.
